title:
Lesson 1:
Not Yet / On My Way
description: 'Todavía no' = not yet (something hasn't happened). 'Ya voy' = I'm coming / on my way. 'Ya' can mean 'already', 'now', or 'right away' depending on context.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Come on, we're waiting for you.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Ya voy, ya voy. Dame dos minutos.
dialogue.english: I'm coming, I'm coming. Give me two minutes.

title:
Lesson 2:
Leaving & Coming Back
description: 'Ya me voy' = I'm leaving now. 'Ya regresé' = I'm back (I already returned, speaker). 'Ya regresó' = he/she is back (third person). 'Ya' signals the action just happened.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I'm leaving now. See you later.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Okay. What time are you coming back?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: I don't know. I'll let you know.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Sí, ya regresó. Y yo ya regresé también.
dialogue.english: Yes, he's back. And I'm already back too.

title:
Lesson 3:
Almost Done / Almost There
description: 'Ya casi' means 'almost' or 'just about'. 'Ya casi termino' = I'm almost done. 'Ya casi llego' = I'm almost there. Very useful when someone is waiting for you.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Where are you? We're waiting for you.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Ya casi llego. Estoy a dos minutos.
dialogue.english: I'm almost there. I'm two minutes away.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: And did you finish the work?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Ya casi termino. Solo me falta un poco.
dialogue.english: I'm almost done. I just have a little left.
